untranslatable_reason: Calendar spread is an options strategy (sell near-term option, buy far-term option at same strike). This requires option-chain data (expirations, implied volatility, time decay) and derivatives pricing logic, not available in the provided price/technical indicator set. The signal cannot be expressed using only OHLCV and technical indicators.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_87 describes a horizontal/calendar time spread strategy. Entry requires: (1) identification of near-term and far-term option expirations, (2) matching strike prices, (3) simultaneous execution of sell/buy. Exit is triggered by near-term expiration. These conditions require options market data (Greeks, time value, volatility surface) beyond the scope of standard price-based backtest indicators. A proper implementation would need an options-aware engine with greeks calculation and time decay modeling.
